feed.title: Ferran Cardoso
feed.subtitle: Ferran Cardoso`s personal profile, portfolio of academical publications, and other outputs. Uses a minimal, responsive, and powerful Jekyll theme for presenting professional writing.

entry.title: Demonstration of our Multimodal Analytical Platform for the Delivery of Integrated Diagnostics in Oncology

link.title: Demonstration of our Multimodal Analytical Platform for the Delivery of Integrated Diagnostics in Oncology

entry.summary: Video Demo Structured Transcript POETIC-Foundry So this is the landing page from the ICR-Foundry accelerator that was done in late 2023. And as you can see, basically, we have here access to certain key applications that we’ll be showcasing in later. And we also have access to some highlighted resources, including the landing page itself, but also the ontology and the filesystem containi...

entry.title: Presenting the IDD platform at the EACR Cancer Multiomics conference

link.title: Presenting the IDD platform at the EACR Cancer Multiomics conference

entry.summary: This is a stub entry discussing my experience at this year’s EACR Cancer Genomics, Multiomics and Computational Biology conference in Bergamo. Building a Platform for Integrative Discovery and Diagnostics in Cancer A PDF copy of this poster’s abstract can be downloaded from here, and cited via DOI: 10.6084/m9.figshare.26414029

entry.title: Graph Signal Processing workshop 2023
entry.summary: This is a stub entry discussing my experience at this year’s Graph Signal Processing workshop in Oxford. I attended together with collaborators Aarthi Venkat and Prof. Smita Krishnaswamy, where we brought work on learning embeddings of directed gene-gene networks. Aarthi presented the work as an amazing talk during the second day! And of course, we also had lots of fun; including a formal di...

entry.title: Big -omic Data and GPU acceleration with CUDA

link.title: Big -omic Data and GPU acceleration with CUDA

entry.summary: As part of the peer-review process of Cardoso Rodriguez & Qin et al. 2023, we decided to compare our murine organoid data with publicly available patient cohorts of Colorectal Cancer. While some of the workflows were suited for the HPC, dataset exploration benefits from the agility of local compute. Now, with Variational AutoEncoder approaches for data integration implemented in PyTorch, m...
